reviews.review_msg: Efe was one of my favourite professors this semester! He explained all class concepts clearly with PowerPoints, but wrote some extra stuff on the whiteboard (his writing was a little messy). Heavy on the reading with three-four academic articles per week; tests focus on these readings a lot. Followed lecture with a modern video. I liked the class!
You can tell he really makes an effort. There is lots of readings, but he did however make 3 hour lecture bearable by including modern content and movies.
Gives good feedback

Honestly an embarrassment for SFU. Most disorganized, confusing, stressful online class I've ever taken. Super vague criteria. Expects students to dedicate their whole lives to a 3 credit course. Midterm took 9 hours and a lot of crying. Don't take it you value your mental health because it's clear he doesn't value the health of students.

The most vague criteria I've ever experienced in any class. Literally gives one line rubrics and always expects students to go so above and beyond for an A grade. Such limited word limits and limited criteria. Words the assignments so confusing too. Ruined my mental health and gave me several breakdowns.

His lectures were not clear and easy to understand. Read the textbook! He made funny jokes in lectures which made everyone giggle. I recommend doing the practice questions he assigns for the midterm to do well. All assignments were graded very strictly making it difficult to get more than a pass. This was the hardest IS course I've taken so far.

Professor Kaya is a good professor! He's passionate about what he's teaching and wants you to succeed. I found the lectures interesting, he sometimes goes off on tangents, but pay attention as he makes very good points. I found the class to be difficult, but fair. Make sure to start working on any research paper he assigns ahead of time.

Not the best lecturer, most lectures are based off reading of slides. Found lectures to be sometimes tedious and long however I do respect the amount of breaks he gave us. Tough grader, probably did the worst I've ever done in an IS course.

My favorite prof in IS so far! He's a really nice guy and I enjoyed his lectures so much. The class was very interesting and I learned a lot from him. Lecture slides are not as detailed so you really have to come to class to take full notes. Overall a great experience for me.

Professor is difficult to listen to (in the sense that his presentation is not engaging). Not as detailed in his lectures in comparison to other IS professors/courses. Attending lectures felt irrelevant and a waste of time. When asked questions he did not seem to be fully equipped to answer them. Nice guy, but not the greatest lecturer.
Awesome and very learned professor.
He's a nice person but a horrible lecturer. The textbook is hard to read. Lecture slides are useless and hard to follow. Too much relies on videos that do not help at all.

Such a great and lovely guy that it makes me sad to say that his teaching is poor. His lectures lose you. Awful slides. Over-reliance on videos - sometimes random television clips. Disconnected tutorials. Weird paper. AWFUL exams with irrelevant questions, absolutely no time and no feedback. Bad textbook and irrelevant readings to top it all off.

Dr. Kaya is an awesome prof! He is funny, organized and is really helpful when answering questions in class or at office hours. He assigns a lot of readings, but you don't need to worry about them too much. Just focus on the readings from the main text and merge it with your lecture notes and you will be good to go!
Class was interesting, Logan is very knowledgeable about international studies. Is a bit of a tough marker.
David was absolutely phenomenal! Easily accessible by email and willing to setup zoom calls outside his office hours. Come to class ready to discuss and you'll learn a ton with him.

Awesome professor. The focus of classes were spread fairly evenly across the world (however, southeastern asia showed up the most often). Plenty of choice was given in terms of paper topics and the midterm and final exams were fair.
Really cultivates engagement in the class. Exceptionally knowledgeable and passionate about what he teaches.
Very engaging professor. He appreciates in-class participation

Is a great lecturer and well organized. 
The content of the course was set up you can retain & add knowledge as you go. The textbook was pretty enjoyable too.
Final exam was super fair, we got the questions in advance.
This is a good prof and course for IS majors, minors or others who need electives.

An outstanding professor who really wants his students to succeed, he is friendly and accessible outside of class. In-class discussions are flexible and interesting, he makes course material easy to learn if you engage with every lecture. The best professor I've ever had at SFU, don't miss the opportunity to take a class with him!

Ok as person he really nice.however, as a prof, his exam long 12-14 short answers + 2 essays (u have to know both books very well), his marking scale so high, B- start from 76. I wouldn't take another class with him nor recommend him for IS210

Very engaging, very knowledgeable professor. He doesn't tend to pull punches, he will challenge you, but not to overload. Smaller class size where conversation is encouraged is where his teaching style fits best. Not everyone clicks with his sense of humour, taken out of context he can be a source of politically incorrect memes.

Absolutely fantastic prof - highly recommended. He provides notes, but make sure to go to the lectures - he's entertaining and explains things beyond the notes, which may help you understand things more.

Don't know what the person below is talking about. The amount of readings was huge, felt like a grad class. Assignment topics were incomprehensible- not sure what he wanted, and he did not give enough time to complete them. Hard to put in genuine effort when all you're trying to do is get assignments done on time.A good academic,but def not a prof.

Good reasonable prof w a sense of humour. Tries to interest students and provides numerous anecdotes and videos. Lack of coordination with TA though. Cut huge chunks of initial syllabus and switched readings,such that one course book (purchased) was not used at all. Overall, fair. Not stellar, but also no huge complaints.

One of the best classes i have taken so far. Though it required a lot of work and readings impossible to finish. Hard work does pay off. Just be sure not to read everything but what it's related to lectures

An AMAZING class and an AMAZING teacher. He does not put his lecture notes on the website so you have to go to classes. There are a lot of readings but they are all very very intereting. If you like to work hard and actually learn something definetely take it!
Be prepared to do some work but it's worth it because you actually focus on specific cases and learn a lot! He is also really cute :-)
Great class.
